types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

fields.name: email

fields.name: lastName

fields.name: organizationNo

fields.name: language
fields.description: The primary language of the entity

fields.name: contactInfo
fields.description: Contact information of the entity

fields.name: address

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

fields.name: hasPreviousPage
fields.description: True if previous pages are available

fields.name: startCursor
fields.description: The global ID of the first element in the page

fields.name: count
fields.description: The number of elements in the page

fields.name: currency

fields.name: totalCost

fields.name: energyCost

fields.name: totalConsumption

fields.name: filtered
fields.description: Number of entries that have been filtered from result set due to empty nodes

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

fields.description: The total price (energy + taxes)

fields.name: energy
fields.description: Nord Pool spot price

fields.name: tax
fields.description: The tax part of the price (guarantee of origin certificate, energy tax (Sweden only) and VAT)

fields.name: startsAt

fields.name: currency

fields.name: level

types.name: PriceLevel
types.description: Price level based on trailing price average (3 days for hourly values and 30 days for daily values)

enumValues.name: NORMAL
enumValues.description: The price is greater than 90 % and less than 115 % compared to average price

enumValues.name: CHEAP
enumValues.description: The price is greater than 60 % and less or equal to 90 % compared to average price

enumValues.name: VERY_CHEAP
enumValues.description: The price is smaller or equal to 60 % compared to average price

enumValues.name: EXPENSIVE
enumValues.description: The price is greater or equal to 115 % and less than 140 % compared to average price

enumValues.name: VERY_EXPENSIVE
enumValues.description: The price is greater or equal to 140 % compared to average price

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: The global ID of the element

fields.name: node
fields.description: A single price node

fields.name: current
fields.description: The energy price right now

fields.name: today
fields.description: The hourly prices of the current day

fields.name: tomorrow
fields.description: The hourly prices of the upcoming day

fields.name: range
fields.description: Range of prices, optionally relative to `before`/`after` arguments. Capped at 744 items (31 days) for resolution HOURLY, and 31 items for resolution DAILY. If you need to fetch a longer interval, perform multiple requests and take advantage of the `pageInfo` data. Do note that prices never change, so store already fetched historical prices locally; if you can use `today`/`tomorrow` instead, use that since the API can serve this data faster and in a more reliable way.

args.name: resolution
args.description: Temporal resolution

args.name: first
args.description: Take the `n` first results from cursor. Cannot be used in conjunction with `last`. Limit count: 744 (HOURLY), 31 (DAILY).

args.name: last
args.description: Take the `n` last results from cursor. Cannot be used in conjunction with `first`. Limit count: 744 (HOURLY), 31 (DAILY).

args.name: before
args.description: Base64-encoded ISO8601 date/time cursor position. Cannot be used in conjunction with `after.` Has no influence when `first` is used.

args.name: after
args.description: Base64-encoded ISO8601 date/time cursor position. Cannot be used in conjunction with `before`. Has no influence when `last` is used.

enumValues.name: NORMAL
enumValues.description: The price is within the range of what is considered being normal (market dependent; see 'priceRating.thresholdPercentages' for limits)

enumValues.name: LOW
enumValues.description: The price is within the range of what is considered being low (market dependent; see 'priceRating.thresholdPercentages' for limits)

enumValues.name: HIGH
enumValues.description: The price is within the range of what is considered being high (market dependent; see 'priceRating.thresholdPercentages' for limits)

fields.name: time
fields.description: The start time of the price

fields.name: energy
fields.description: Nord Pool spot price

fields.name: total
fields.description: The total price (incl. tax)

fields.name: tax
fields.description: The tax part of the price (guarantee of origin certificate, energy tax (Sweden only) and VAT)

fields.name: difference
fields.description: The percentage difference compared to the trailing price average (1 day for 'hourly', 30 days for 'daily' and 32 months for 'monthly')

fields.name: level
fields.description: The price level compared to recent price values (calculated using 'difference' and 'priceRating.thresholdPercentages')

fields.name: minEnergy
fields.description: Lowest Nord Pool spot price over the time period

fields.name: maxEnergy
fields.description: Highest Nord Pool spot price over the time period

fields.name: minTotal
fields.description: Lowest total price (incl. tax) over the time period

fields.name: maxTotal
fields.description: Highest total price (incl. tax) over the time period

fields.name: currency
fields.description: The price currency

fields.name: entries
fields.description: The individual price entries aggregated by hourly/daily/monthly values

fields.name: high
fields.description: The percentage difference when the price is considered to be 'high' (market dependent)

fields.name: low
fields.description: The percentage difference when the price is considered to be 'low' (market dependent)

fields.name: thresholdPercentages
fields.description: The different 'high'/'low' price breakpoints (market dependent)

fields.name: hourly
fields.description: The hourly prices of today, the previous 7 days, and tomorrow

fields.name: daily
fields.description: The daily prices of today and the previous 30 days

fields.name: monthly
fields.description: The monthly prices of this month and the previous 31 months

fields.name: subscriber
fields.description: The owner of the subscription

fields.name: validFrom
fields.description: The time the subscription started

fields.name: validTo
fields.description: The time the subscription ended

fields.name: status
fields.description: The current status of the subscription

fields.name: priceInfo
fields.description: Price information related to the subscription. For backward-compatible reasons, you have to set `resolution` to `QUARTER_HOURLY` to get quarter-hourly prices.

fields.name: priceInfoRange
fields.description: Historical price information related to the subscription. Replaces `priceInfo.range`. Range of prices, optionally relative to `before`/`after` arguments. Capped at 672 items (7 days) for resolution QUARTER_HOURLY, 744 items (31 days) for resolution HOURLY, and 31 items for resolution DAILY. If you need to fetch a longer interval, perform multiple requests and take advantage of the `pageInfo` data. Do note that prices never change, so store already fetched historical prices locally; if you can use `today`/`tomorrow` instead, use that since the API can serve this data faster and in a more reliable way.

args.name: resolution
args.description: Temporal resolution

args.name: first
args.description: Take the `n` first results from cursor. Cannot be used in conjunction with `last`. Limit count: 672 (QUARTER_HOURLY), 744 (HOURLY), 31 (DAILY).

args.name: last
args.description: Take the `n` last results from cursor. Cannot be used in conjunction with `first`. Limit count: 672 (QUARTER_HOURLY), 744 (HOURLY), 31 (DAILY).

args.name: before
args.description: Base64-encoded ISO8601 date/time cursor position. Cannot be used in conjunction with `after.` Has no influence when `first` is used.

args.name: after
args.description: Base64-encoded ISO8601 date/time cursor position. Cannot be used in conjunction with `before`. Has no influence when `last` is used.

fields.name: priceRating
fields.description: Alternative price information related to the subscription (deprecated)

fields.name: currency
fields.description: The cost currency

fields.name: consumption
fields.description: Consumption connection. Capped at 744 items (31 days) for resolution HOURLY, 31 items for DAILY, 52 items for WEEKLY, 12 items for MONTHLY and 1 for ANNUAL. If you need to fetch a longer interval, perform multiple requests and take advantage of the `pageInfo` data. Do note that this data is not updated in real-time, so try not to fetch this continually if there is no need to; and try to cache/store historical data locally.

args.name: first
args.description: Take the `n` first results from cursor. Cannot be used in conjunction with `last`. Limit count: 744 (HOURLY), 31 (DAILY), 52 (WEEKLY), 12 (MONTHLY), 1 (ANNUAL).

args.name: last
args.description: Take the `n` last results from cursor. Cannot be used in conjunction with `first`. Limit count: 744 (HOURLY), 31 (DAILY), 52 (WEEKLY), 12 (MONTHLY), 1 (ANNUAL).

args.name: before
args.description: Base64-encoded ISO8601 date/time cursor start. Cannot be used in conjunction with `after`. Has no influence when `first` is used.

args.name: after
args.description: Base64-encoded ISO8601 date/time cursor start. Cannot be used in conjunction with `before`. Has no influence when `last` is used.

args.name: filterEmptyNodes
args.description: Whether to include empty nodes. Default value: false

fields.name: production
fields.description: Production connection. Capped at 744 items (31 days) for resolution HOURLY, 31 items for DAILY, 52 items for WEEKLY, 12 items for MONTHLY and 1 for ANNUAL. If you need to fetch a longer interval, perform multiple requests and take advantage of the `pageInfo` data. Do note that this data is not updated in real-time, so try not to fetch this continually if there is no need to; and try to cache/store historical data locally.

args.name: first
args.description: Take the `n` first results from cursor. Cannot be used in conjunction with `last`. Limit count: 744 (HOURLY), 31 (DAILY), 52 (WEEKLY), 12 (MONTHLY), 1 (ANNUAL).

args.name: last
args.description: Take the `n` last results from cursor. Cannot be used in conjunction with `first`. Limit count: 744 (HOURLY), 31 (DAILY), 52 (WEEKLY), 12 (MONTHLY), 1 (ANNUAL).

args.name: before
args.description: Base64-encoded ISO8601 date/time cursor start. Cannot be used in conjunction with `after`. Has no influence when `first` is used.

args.name: after
args.description: Base64-encoded ISO8601 date/time cursor start. Cannot be used in conjunction with `before`. Has no influence when `last` is used.

args.name: filterEmptyNodes
args.description: Whether to include empty nodes. Default value: false

types.name: LiveMeasurement
types.description: Real-time measurement data from Pulse or Watty device. For meter connected Pulses, the data update rate is governed by the meter, but may be throttled/batched by Tibber. Differences in meter hardware and/or firmware means that it is not guaranteed that all fields are present in every data frame and furthermore cannot be guaranteed to be fully correct or complete, given that Tibber relies on what the meter provides. Tibber does not normalize or modify the meter data other than to try to handle known meter/firmware bugs and idiosyncrasies. Meter data may be enriched with additional aggregated fields where possible.

fields.name: timestamp
fields.description: Timestamp when usage occurred

fields.name: power
fields.description: Consumption at the moment in watts (W)

fields.name: lastMeterConsumption
fields.description: Last meter active import register state in kilowatt-hours (kWh)

fields.name: accumulatedConsumption
fields.description: Energy consumed since midnight in kilowatt-hours (kWh)

fields.name: accumulatedProduction
fields.description: Net energy produced since midnight in kilowatt-hours (kWh)

fields.name: accumulatedConsumptionLastHour
fields.description: Energy consumed since last hour shift in kilowatt-hours (kWh)

fields.name: accumulatedProductionLastHour
fields.description: Net energy produced since last hour shift in kilowatt-hours (kWh)

fields.name: accumulatedCost
fields.description: Accumulated cost since midnight; requires active Tibber power deal; includes VAT (where applicable)

fields.name: accumulatedReward
fields.description: Accumulated production reward since midnight; requires active Tibber power deal. Note that this is unrelated to Grid Rewards.

fields.name: currency
fields.description: Currency of displayed cost; requires active Tibber power deal

fields.name: minPower
fields.description: Minimum power consumption since midnight in watts (W)

fields.name: averagePower
fields.description: Average power consumption since midnight in watts (W)

fields.name: maxPower
fields.description: Peak power consumption since midnight in watts (W)

fields.name: powerProduction
fields.description: Net power production (A-) at the moment in watts (W)

fields.name: powerReactive
fields.description: Reactive power consumption (Q+) at the moment (kVAr)

fields.name: powerProductionReactive
fields.description: Net reactive power production (Q-) at the moment (kVAr)

fields.name: minPowerProduction
fields.description: Minimum net power production since midnight in watts (W)

fields.name: maxPowerProduction
fields.description: Maximum net power production since midnight in watts (W)

fields.name: lastMeterProduction
fields.description: Last meter active export register state in kilowatt-hours (kWh)

fields.name: powerFactor
fields.description: Power factor (active power / apparent power); 0 to 1 where 1 is 100 %

fields.name: voltagePhase1
fields.description: Voltage on phase 1; on some meters, the value is not part of every data frame, therefore the value can be `null` at certain intervals. It is not guaranteed that single phase installations will be reporting on Phase 1 since Tibber does not normalize this data and only reports what is received from the meter.

fields.name: voltagePhase2
fields.description: Voltage on phase 2; on some meters, the value is not part of every data frame, therefore the value can be `null` at certain intervals. It is not guaranteed that single phase installations will be reporting on Phase 1 since Tibber does not normalize this data and only reports what is received from the meter.

fields.name: voltagePhase3
fields.description: Voltage on phase 3; on some meters, the value is not part of every data frame, therefore the value can be `null` at certain intervals. It is not guaranteed that single phase installations will be reporting on Phase 1 since Tibber does not normalize this data and only reports what is received from the meter.

fields.name: currentL1
fields.description: Current on L1; on some meters, the value is not part of every data frame, therefore the value can be `null` at certain intervals. It is not guaranteed that single phase installations will be reporting on Phase 1 since Tibber does not normalize this data and only reports what is received from the meter.

fields.name: currentL2
fields.description: Current on L2; on some meters, the value is not part of every data frame, therefore the value can be `null` at certain intervals. It is not guaranteed that single phase installations will be reporting on Phase 1 since Tibber does not normalize this data and only reports what is received from the meter.

fields.name: currentL3
fields.description: Current on L3; on some meters, the value is not part of every data frame, therefore the value can be `null` at certain intervals. It is not guaranteed that single phase installations will be reporting on Phase 1 since Tibber does not normalize this data and only reports what is received from the meter.

fields.name: signalStrength
fields.description: Device signal strength (Pulse: dB(m); Watty: percent)

fields.name: sendMeterReading
fields.description: Deprecated; calling this mutation will do nothing

fields.name: updateHome
fields.description: Update home information

fields.name: sendPushNotification
fields.description: Send notification to Tibber app on registered devices

fields.name: liveMeasurement
fields.description: Subscribe to real-time measurement stream from Pulse or Watty device

fields.name: testMeasurement
fields.description: Subscribe to test stream

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Included when true.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates exactly one field must be supplied and this field must not be `null`.
